November 30, 2000
Honorable Gail Benda
City Councilmember
City of Imperial Beach
825 Imperial Beach Boulevard
Imperial Beach, CA 91932
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-00-247
Dear Councilmember B enda:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding your duties as an
Fl*ul Beach City Councilmember under the conflict-of-inierest provisionsl of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").2
QUESTION
May you participate in city council deliberations and decisions concerning the
city's evalualion of existing shore protection structures and possible implementation of a
new overall shoreline management strategy?
CONCLUSION
You may not participate in city council deliberations and decisions concerning the
city's evaluation of existing shore protection structures and possible implementation of a
new overall shoreline management strategy due to your ownership of real property on the
oceanfront
I Please note that the Commission is currently considering various amendments to the conflict-of-
interest regulations discussed below. You may wish to monitor thl progress of these amendments on the
Commission's website (www.fppc.ca.gov). The advice contained herein is based on the law existing as of
the issue daG of this letter.
2 Government Code sections 81000 - 91015. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-00-247
Page No. 2
You are a member ofthe Imperial Beach city council. you also own rear
properfy at 1324 Seacoast Drive, *hi.h is located ; th; lceanfront in the city of
Imperial Beach' In septemb er 1,'999,the city submitteJan application to the bahfornia
coastal commission for purposes of obtaining a sr*ia ."lg1.t a coastal engineering
study' The grant was subsequently awarrlea ilatI" city soticitea proposals for coastal
engineering services to conduct u ttudy of the existing;ilore protection skuctures along
the oceanfront in order to develop an overall shorefinE management strategy in Imperial
Beach' An agreement for the conduct of such u rtuJy*u, executed in July 2000.
The purpose of the study is to:
a) Develop an understanding of the various kinds of shore protection devices that
have been constructed in Imperial Beach;
b) Develop, if needed, an implementation plan that presents standards for new
devices, repair of existing devices, and calculation of sand mitigation fees when
determined appropriate;
c) Develop' if needed, a Shoreline Protection ordinance that considers local beach
conditions and existing development patterns; and
d) coordinate these procedures, if adopted, with the Local coastal program.
Public workshops ar: an integral part of the engineering study and the first of
such workshops was set for Novembl g, ZOOO.
FACTS
ANALYSIS

following eight-step analysis.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May you participate in city council deliberations and decisions concerning the
city's evalualion of existing shore protection structures and possible implementation of a
new overall shoreline management strategy?

CONCLUSION: You may not participate in city council deliberations and decisions concerning the
city's evaluation of existing shore protection structures and possible implementation of a
new overall shoreline management strategy due to your ownership of real property on the
oceanfront
I Please note that the Commission is currently considering various amendments to the conflict-of-
interest regulations discussed below. You may wish to monitor thl progress of these amendments on the
Commission's website (www.fppc.ca.gov). The advice contained herein is based on the law existing as of
the issue daG of this letter.
2Page No. 2
You are a member ofthe Imperial Beach city council. you also own rear
properfy at 1324 Seacoast Drive, *hi.h is located ; th; lceanfront in the city of
Imperial Beach' In septemb er 1,'999,the city submitteJan application to the bahfornia
coastal commission for purposes of obtaining a sr*ia ."lg1.t a coastal engineering
study' The grant was subsequently awarrlea ilatI" city soticitea proposals for coastal
engineering services to conduct u ttudy of the existing;ilore protection skuctures along
the oceanfront in order to develop an overall shorefinE management strategy in Imperial
Beach' An agreement for the conduct of such u rtuJy*u, executed in July 2000.
The purpose of the study is to:
a) Develop an understanding of the various kinds of shore protection devices that
have been constructed in Imperial Beach;
b) Develop, if needed, an implementation plan that presents standards for new
devices, repair of existing devices, and calculation of sand mitigation fees when
determined appropriate;
c) Develop' if needed, a Shoreline Protection ordinance that considers local beach
conditions and existing development patterns; and
d) coordinate these procedures, if adopted, with the Local coastal program.
Public workshops ar: an integral part of the engineering study and the first of

CONCLUSION You may not participate in city council deliberations and decisions concerning the city's evaluation of existing shore protection structures and possible implementation of a new overall shoreline management strategy due to your ownership of real property on the oceanfront I Please note that the Commission is currently considering various amendments to the conflict-of- interest regulations discussed below. You may wish to monitor thl progress of these amendments on the Commission's website (www.fppc.ca.gov). The advice contained herein is based on the law existing as of the issue daG of this letter. 2 Government Code sections 81000 - 91015. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations. The purpose of the study is to: a) Develop an understanding of the various kinds of shore protection devices that have been constructed in Imperial Beach; b) Develop, if needed, an implementation plan that presents standards for new devices, repair of existing devices, and calculation of sand mitigation fees when determined appropriate; c) Develop' if needed, a Shoreline Protection ordinance that considers local beach conditions and existing development patterns; and d) coordinate these procedures, if adopted, with the Local coastal program.
